title: VinFast, Alcoa, And Regencell Are Among the Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (March 30-April 2): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: U.S. large-cap stocks posted strong gains during the shortened week of March 30-April 2. VinFast Auto led with a 42.77% jump driven by strong EV momentum and Canadian rebates. Alcoa gained 24.12% on bullish commodities sentiment amid Middle East conflict resolution expectations. Other notable gainers included SBA Communications (23.22%), Equinox Gold (20.98%), and several precious metals miners, with gains ranging from 15-19%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Jumped 42.77% this week driven by strong EV momentum domestically and increased demand for VF 8 in North America boosted by Canadian rebates

title: Whale Watch: Major International Portfolio Manager Fully Exits SBA Communications Position
author: Jake Lerch
description: Ausbil Investment Management Ltd completely sold its entire 15,483 share stake in SBA Communications, valued at $3.64 million, likely due to the company's poor stock performance over the past year.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock declined 21.38% over the past year, significantly underperformed S&P 500 by 38.10 percentage points, and lost institutional investor support as demonstrated by Ausbil's complete exit

title: Millicom (Tigo) Q2 2025 Earnings Release
author: Millicom International Cellular S.A.
description: Millicom reported Q2 2025 revenue of $1.37 billion, record adjusted EBITDA of $641 million, and net profit of $676 million. The company completed a partial infrastructure transaction with SBA, generating over $500 million in proceeds and announced a $2.50 per share interim dividend.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned as a transaction partner in infrastructure deal with no specific performance details
Briefly mentioned as a potential acquisition target in Uruguay and Ecuador with no additional context
Strong financial performance with record adjusted EBITDA, significant infrastructure transaction proceeds, strategic expansion through potential acquisitions in Uruguay and Ecuador

title: Vytautas Vaškys perrinktas „Utenos trikotažo“ valdybos pirmininku
author: N/A
description: Utenos Trikotažas, a textile group owned by SBA, has seen a significant improvement in its financial performance in the first quarter of 2025. The company's sales increased by 66.3% compared to the same period last year, reaching 5.2 million euros. Additionally, the group's losses were reduced by almost one million euros, from 1.48 million euros in the first quarter of 2024 to 440 thousand euros in the same period of 2025.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: SBA is the owner of Utenos Trikotažas, and the article suggests that the group's involvement has contributed to the positive performance of the textile company. This implies that SBA's management and strategic decisions have been effective in turning around the company's fortunes.
